title: lolium rigidum var. subulatum
intro: Lolium rigidum var. subulatum, also known as rigid ryegrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and aiding in digestion, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Lolium rigidum var. subulatum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
